pattern: Since the single allowed operation can bump any subset of characters, a greedy left-to-right match that accepts either the original or the bumped character is always at least as good as any smarter strategy.
hints: You never have to decide up front which characters to bump; deciding as you scan str1 left to right and matching str2 greedily works just as well.
At each character of str1, ask whether it (unchanged) or its cyclic successor equals the character str2 is currently waiting for, and advance str2's pointer the moment either one matches.
'z' cycles back to 'a', not to '{'; once str2's pointer reaches the end of str2 you are done, whether or not str1 has more characters left.

edge_inputs.description: one bump at the end is enough
every character of str1 needs to be bumped
no bump can reach the target letter
'z' must wrap to 'a', not fail or overflow
str2 already matches without needing any bump at all
single character str1 and str2, exact match with no bump needed
single character str1 and str2, match only via one bump
str2 is longer than what str1 can ever satisfy as a subsequence
greedy must not bump a character it does not need to, saving the option for later

stress.description: 100k-length str1 and str2 of matching case-shifted letters, exercising the full greedy scan
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    str1 = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(0, 26)) for _ in range(n))
    m = max(1, n // 2)
    str2_chars = []
    for _ in range(m):
        c = chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(0, 26))
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            c = chr(ord('a') + (ord(c) - ord('a') + 1) % 26)
        str2_chars.append(c)
    return [str1, ''.join(str2_chars)]

stress.description: 100k identical 'a' characters in str1 against a str2 that alternates needing a bump or not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    str1 = 'a' * n
    m = max(1, n // 3)
    str2 = ''.join('a' if i % 2 == 0 else 'b' for i in range(m))
    return [str1, str2]
